description:
You are acting as an infrastructure configuration-manager who needs to take a
  very small “snapshot” of the current state of the machine for later
  comparison.  Collect five basic system facts and save them in a single,
  easy-to-diff log file.

  1. Create the directory  
     /home/user/config_state  
     if it does not already exist.

  2. Inside that directory create **exactly one new file** whose name follows
     this scheme (all in UTC, 24-hour clock):

        state_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log

     • YYYY   – 4-digit year  
     • MM     – 2-digit month  
     • DD     – 2-digit day  
     • HH     – 2-digit hour  
     • MM     – 2-digit minute  
     • SS     – 2-digit second  

     Example:  state_20240105_090213.log

  3. Populate the file with the following five lines, **in the exact order
     shown, one key=value pair per line, no additional whitespace, no percent
     symbols, no units, and a single trailing newline after the last line**:

        DATE=<ISO-8601-date-only>        (format: YYYY-MM-DD, same date used in filename)
        HOSTNAME=<result_of_hostname>    (plain hostname)
        KERNEL=<result_of_uname_-r>       (kernel release string)
        MEM_TOTAL_MB=<integer>           (total RAM in megabytes, obtained from /proc/meminfo MemTotal, always round down)
        ROOT_USAGE_PERCENT=<integer>     (root filesystem used-percentage as reported by “df /”, but write only the number, e.g. 12)

     Example of correct final content (values will differ on your machine):

        DATE=2024-01-05
        HOSTNAME=workstation01
        KERNEL=5.15.0-91-generic
        MEM_TOTAL_MB=7859
        ROOT_USAGE_PERCENT=41

  4. Do not write anything else to stdout other than the normal command
     outputs you use to verify your work.  The automated grader will look only
     at the existence, name pattern, and *exact* file content.

  Hints (do NOT paste these commands, they are only guidance):
    • `hostname`, `uname -r`, `cat /proc/meminfo | grep MemTotal`, `df /`
    • Remember to convert kB → MB: divide by 1024 and round down (integer).

  When you are done, the directory should contain one and only one *.log*
  file whose name matches the stated pattern, and that file must have the
  five keys in the precise order and format above.
truth:
<!--
    The grader will:

      • Verify directory /home/user/config_state exists.
      • Locate a single file matching regex: state_\d{8}_\d{6}\.log
      • Open that file and read exactly 5 non-empty lines.
      • Validate that each line matches the following regex sequence:

          1) ^DATE=\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\n$
          2) ^HOSTNAME=[A-Za-z0-9._-]+\n$
          3) ^KERNEL=.+\n$
          4) ^MEM_TOTAL_MB=\d+\n$
          5) ^ROOT_USAGE_PERCENT=\d+\n$

      • Independently compute:

          mem_total_mb = floor( MemTotal(kB) / 1024 )
          root_usage   = the numeric part of the “Use%” field from `df /`

        and compare against lines 4 and 5.

      • Confirm that the DATE value equals the UTC date used in the filename.

      • Confirm there are no additional characters, spaces, or blank lines.

    No static secret values are needed because all references come from the
    live system at evaluation time.  The critical verification items are:

      directory_path   = /home/user/config_state
      filename_pattern = state_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.log
  -->
name: task_000000_82173a03
